subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Burns and Skin Layers
description: A fast breakdown of Burns and Skin Layers for builders.

hook.text: A first-degree burn hurts terribly. A third-degree burn is painless. That's terrifying.

buildup.text: Skin has three main layers: epidermis, dermis, and subcutaneous tissue.

discovery.text: First-degree burns damage only the epidermis. Third-degree burns destroy all layers.

twist.text: Third-degree burns are painless because the nerve endings have been destroyed.

climax.text: The body can regenerate epidermis, but full-thickness burns need grafts to heal.

punchline.text: More pain can mean less damage. A paradox that guides first aid.
